TechnologyStack.Runtime: CPython 3.12 under systemd on a Linux VPS
TechnologyStack.Framework: WSGI over the Python standard library for the localhost admin API, with MCP-capable clients calling the backend over authenticated loopback HTTP and using a local MCP stdio server for the approved tool surface.

dep.name: nanobot
dep.purpose: One supported MCP-capable agent integration example; not part of the required runtime boundary

dep.name: jinja2
dep.purpose: Config or policy templating if rendered file generation is required

dep.name: typer
dep.purpose: Local admin CLI wrapper for non-MCP maintenance operations

dep.name: occtl
dep.purpose: Session and control-plane command interface for ocserv

dep.name: systemctl
dep.purpose: Service management and reload invocation under Linux

dep.name: sudo
dep.purpose: Privilege boundary for approved administrative operations

constraint.reason: Implementation should target Python 3.12 semantics and typing surface.

module-level.focus: Fast deterministic unit and contract checks for adapters, action validation, guard logic, and the MCP server.

wave-level.focus: Integration checks for merged surfaces such as MCP-to-backend routing and ocserv control flows.

phase-level.focus: Broader regression confidence across user, group, session, reload, and rollback paths.

Testing.mocking-policy: Mock subprocess and system calls narrowly, use temp directories for config rendering tests, and prefer narrow fake adapters or local app clients over broad monkeypatching.

Observability.logger: JSON-lines audit logging through the Python standard library
Observability.required-fields: timestamp, level, event, request_id, actor_type, actor_id, command, target_user, target_group, result, reload_status, error_code
Observability.redaction: Never log secrets, backend auth tokens, passwords, cert material, session cookies, or full config bodies. Audit logs should record action intent and result, not credentials.

DeliveryShape.shape: MCP-capable clients talk only to a constrained localhost WSGI backend over bearer-authenticated loopback HTTP and use a local MCP stdio server that exposes only the approved ocserv administration tools.
